_comment_mode: CRITICAL: 'mode' controls paper vs live trading. NEVER flip to 'live' in a PR. Live mode requires a separate, manually-created .claude/settings.local.json and operator presence. See CLAUDE.md hard rule #1.
_comment_model: Default model for the main Claude Code supervisor session. Subagents override via their own frontmatter.
_comment_permissions: Allow-list of tool calls the supervisor can make without prompting. Everything else requires confirmation. Subagents inherit NONE of this — they have their own tool lists in their frontmatter.
_comment_hooks: Hooks fire on specific lifecycle events regardless of LLM decisions. They are the enforcement mechanism for safety properties that must be guaranteed.

PreToolUse.description: Blocks order submission if mode != paper or if hard risk limits are violated. Hard gate — cannot be bypassed by the LLM.
Refuses any broker call that points at live Binance endpoints while mode=paper.

PostToolUse.description: After order submission completes, updates positions and writes a row to nav_snapshots. Ensures the audit log stays in sync even if the session crashes.

SessionStart.description: On every session start, verifies .env exists, DATABASE_URL is reachable, and current mode matches what the operator expects.

_comment_mcp: Postgres MCP for ad-hoc reads (SELECT only). All structured INSERTs go through desk/db.py so the audit log is consistent.

postgres-readonly._comment: This server must be configured as read-only in its own wrapper script if your MCP implementation does not enforce it. Writes from the supervisor must go through desk/db.py, not this MCP.

_comment_defaults: Project-wide defaults for subagent invocation. Individual subagent files override.
_comment_telemetry: No telemetry beacons from Parley itself. Anthropic's standard Claude Code telemetry is separate and unaffected.
